sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a history of type 2 diabetes and hypertension. He denies swelling, carotid bruits, or thyromegaly. His blood sugars have been elevated, as indicated by an elevated hemoglobin A1c.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals a grade 2 systolic ejection murmur. Pulses are normal. Blood pressure is currently well-controlled. Hemoglobin A1c is elevated.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's primary problems are type 2 diabetes and hypertension. His blood sugars are out of control.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: The patient should continue taking lisinopril 10 mg daily. We want the hemoglobin A1c to be under 7%. The patient should check his blood sugars in the morning and call in about two weeks to report them. Follow up in approximately two months to assess blood sugar control.
